title: Docs + tests for existing module: document and cover an untested parser
strength: Comprehension of existing code, then producing accurate docs and meaningful coverage.
prompt: src/csv.js is an existing, working CSV parser with NO tests and NO documentation. It exports parseCSV(text, {delimiter=',', headers=true}) and stringifyCSV(rows, {delimiter=','}). The parser handles quoted fields, escaped double-quotes ("" inside a quoted field), embedded newlines inside quotes, and a configurable delimiter. Do two things WITHOUT changing src/csv.js: (1) Write docs/csv.md documenting both functions — signature, parameters, return shape, and at least two worked examples each (input -> output), including how quoting and escaping behave. (2) Write a thorough Jest test suite in test/csv.test.js that covers: simple parse with headers, parse without headers (array of arrays), quoted fields containing the delimiter, escaped quotes, embedded newlines, a round-trip (stringify then parse returns the original rows), and custom delimiter (e.g. ';'). All tests must pass against the EXISTING implementation — read the code to learn its exact behaviour rather than assuming.
verify: git diff --quiet -- src/csv.js || (echo 'implementation was modified - not allowed' && false)
